title: Service Account Brute Force Followed by AD Reconnaissance
short_description: A burst of failed Windows logons against a service account succeeded on attempt 48, immediately followed by Active Directory reconnaissance commands.

alert.rule_name: Authentication - Successful Login Following Burst of Failures
alert.description: 47 failed logon attempts (EventCode=4625) for svc_backup from 10.0.4.55 within 3 minutes, followed by a successful logon (EventCode=4624).
alert.raw: 06/12/2026 03:07:00 WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624 host=DC01 Account_Name=svc_backup Source_Network_Address=10.0.4.55 Logon_Type=3

steps.title: Pull authentication events for svc_backup around the alert time
steps.spl: search index=wineventlog host="DC01" Account_Name="svc_backup" (EventCode=4625 OR EventCode=4624) earliest="2026-06-12T03:00:00" latest="2026-06-12T03:10:00" | stats count(eval(EventCode=4625)) as failed_logons count(eval(EventCode=4624)) as successful_logons earliest(_time) as first_attempt latest(_time) as last_attempt values(Source_Network_Address) as src_ip by Account_Name

events.note: 47x 4625 between 03:03:58-03:06:47, then a single 4624 at 03:06:51 - the 48th attempt succeeded.

steps.title: Look up asset context for source IP 10.0.4.55

result.category: Guest Wi-Fi DHCP pool (172.16.0.0/22, NAT'd to 10.0.4.0/24)
result.notes: First seen on the network 2 days ago. Not a known administrative workstation; svc_backup has never authenticated from this subnet before.

steps.title: Look up identity context for svc_backup

result.notes: Used exclusively by Veeam backup jobs. Has never authenticated interactively (Logon_Type=3 from a workstation subnet) before this event.

steps.title: Search for post-logon activity by svc_backup after 03:06:51
steps.spl: search index=wineventlog host="DC01" Account_Name="svc_backup" earliest="2026-06-12T03:06:51" latest="2026-06-12T03:15:00" (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=5145) | table _time, EventCode, Account_Name, Process_Command_Line, Share_Name, Relative_Target_Name

steps.title: Trace the DHCP lease for 10.0.4.55 to a device owner
steps.spl: search index=network sourcetype=dhcp ip="10.0.4.55" earliest=-3d | table _time, ip, mac, hostname, lease_event | join mac [| inputlookup byod_registry.csv]

events.note: Contractor's badge access was revoked on offboarding, but their RADIUS/Wi-Fi credentials were never disabled - a 7-day gap.

ground_truth.narrative: An offboarded contractor's still-active Wi-Fi/RADIUS credentials (revoked badge access on 2026-06-05, but network credentials left enabled) were used to connect to the guest Wi-Fi pool and brute-force the svc_backup service account - a high-value account that has never authenticated interactively and whose password had not been rotated in 412 days. The 48th attempt succeeded, and within seconds the session ran 'Get-ADUser -Filter * -Properties *' and enumerated \\DC01\NETLOGON - classic Active Directory reconnaissance that typically precedes lateral movement or a Kerberoasting/DCSync follow-up. This is an active credential-access incident with a clear privilege-escalation trajectory, not a benign misconfiguration.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable network/RADIUS credentials for temp_contractor_alex (offboarding gap).
Force-rotate the svc_backup password and migrate it to a vaulted/managed service account (e.g. gMSA).
Isolate source host 10.0.4.55 and block its MAC address (3C:22:FB:9A:11:E4) at the WLAN controller.
Review every action taken under svc_backup since 03:06:51 for signs of lateral movement, DCSync, or Kerberoasting.
Lower the account lockout threshold and enable impossible-travel / first-time-source alerting for service accounts.
Audit the contractor offboarding checklist to ensure Wi-Fi/RADIUS access is revoked alongside badge access going forward.
